linkedin_post:
🚨 **Canada's AMR landscape is shifting: New federal procurement and global mandates are opening massive strategic doors for health-tech innovators.**

The International Development Research Centre (IDRC) has launched an RFP for evaluation services for the InnoVet-AMR 2.0 program. This 5-month contract offers a vital entry point into understanding the gaps in Canada's efforts against Antimicrobial Resistance, directly aligning with [CIHR](https://cihr-irsc.gc.ca/)'s 'One Health' AMR research networks. Health-tech consultancies and simulation software developers should target this evaluation bid to position their virtual modeling capabilities for subsequent high-value simulation opportunities.

Simultaneously, the World Health Assembly has adopted the updated Global Action Plan on Antimicrobial Resistance (2026–2036). This 10-year global framework's heavy emphasis on 'One Health' surveillance and innovation creates a long-term commercial runway for predictive analytics. Canadian biotech firms specializing in infectious disease modeling must pay attention now to align their custom pathogen simulation tools with derived national funding opportunities.

Full dashboard with filters and strategic analysis 👉 https://4mayAi.github.io/canadian-grant-intelligence/amr-simulation/

#AMR #OneHealth #Biotech #CanadaProcurement #HealthTech

### Featured News & Sources
- [Evaluation Services for the InnoVet-AMR 2.0 program](https://canadabuys.canada.ca/tender-opportunities/tender-notice/cb-621-79539261) (Canada CanadaBuys Procurement)
- [The World Health Assembly adopts updated Global Action Plan on Antimicrobial Resistance (2026–2036) - World Health Organization (WHO)](https://www.who.int/news/item/25-05-2026-the-world-health-assembly-adopts-updated-global-action-plan-on-antimicrobial-resistance-(2026-2036)) (Canada CIHR AMR)

insights.title: Evaluation Services for the InnoVet-AMR 2.0 program

insight.linkedin_hook: 🔍 New RFP from IDRC for InnoVet-AMR 2.0 evaluation services! This 5-month contract offers a key opportunity to shape the future of Antimicrobial Resistance initiatives. #AMR #PublicHealth #CanadaProcurement
insight.strategic_value:
- This RFP for evaluation services for the InnoVet-AMR 2.0 program presents a strategic entry point into understanding Canada's efforts against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR), directly aligning with CIHR's 'One Health' AMR research networks that fund virtual modeling and bacterial simulation projects (Fact ID 1000).
- Participating in the evaluation provides an invaluable opportunity to gain insights into program effectiveness, data collection methodologies, and future needs, positioning a firm for subsequent bids involving simulation software or advanced data analytics for infectious disease modeling (Fact ID 1002).
- **Consulting Pivot:** Propose a specialized consulting service to analyze InnoVet-AMR 2.0's evaluation outcomes, identifying gaps where custom pathogen simulation tools or advanced virtual modeling could enhance future AMR interventions and program impact measurement.
insight.co_bidding_opportunity: Yes, the solicitation explicitly encourages partnering, stating: "Are you interested in partnering with other businesses for this opportunity? Add your company to the list of businesses who are interested in partnering." Currently listed interested parties include: CABINET EXPERTISE COMMUNAUTAIRE, Nafsta Ltd, ADVOCACY ONG Bénin Aid, Reach medical mission, Association Maison de Vie, BAMBAT Innovative Solution Research Consult, Groupement Agropastoral pour le Développement de Yongoro, ONG NATIONAL, Dutch Committee for Afghanistan - Livestock Programs, and RIDING-UP SARL.
insight.mets_rationalization: Securing evaluation services for InnoVet-AMR 2.0 provides a strategic foothold within public health initiatives combating antimicrobial resistance. This directly supports the long-term national health security agenda and creates pathways to integrate advanced virtual modeling and simulation expertise (as funded by CIHR's 'One Health' networks - Fact ID 1000, and PHAC's infectious disease modeling - Fact ID 1002) into future phases or related programs based on evaluation findings.

insights.title: The World Health Assembly adopts updated Global Action Plan on Antimicrobial Resistance (2026–2036) - World Health Organization (WHO)

insight.linkedin_hook: The WHO's updated Global Action Plan on AMR (2026-2036) is a monumental call to action! 🌍 This "One Health" framework demands advanced tools for surveillance and prevention.
insight.strategic_value:
- This global framework reinforces the critical need for advanced simulation and modeling tools to address Antimicrobial Resistance across human, animal, and environmental health sectors.
- The emphasis on "strengthened surveillance" and "innovation" under the 'One Health' approach directly supports opportunities for Canadian biotech firms in predictive analytics and pathogen simulation.
- * **Consulting Pivot:** Engage Canadian health agencies and academic institutions to align custom pathogen simulation and virtual modeling solutions with national action plans derived from the GAP-AMR, targeting CIHR's 'One Health' networks.
insight.co_bidding_opportunity: The plan emphasizes "continued collaboration among governments, international organizations, civil society, academia and the private sector." This suggests opportunities for consortia involving academic research institutions, biotech firms specializing in simulation, and public health bodies.
insight.mets_rationalization: The updated GAP-AMR establishes a long-term global and national regulatory framework for AMR. This directly creates demand for digital tools, like simulation software, to fulfill the "One Health" and "strengthened surveillance" mandates, aligning with CIHR's explicit funding for virtual modeling (Fact ID 1000).
